title: Academy
summary: Academy drives research; cannot research while upgrading; duty slot at Lv20; Keep 2 to start.
content: Academy unlocks Keep 2; caps out Lv50 (Keep 50 required). Cannot start new research while Academy itself is upgrading (menu selectable but not startable); Skill Books Shop and Duty usable during upgrade. Milestones: Lv17 Research>Recovery; Lv20 Duty (duty officer slot); Lv28 Research>Military Advance; Lv33 Research>Defense Advance; Lv35 enables completing all Advancement-Military Advance categories; Lv40 enables completing all Defense Advance; Lv41 unlocks Research>Offensive/Defensive Mastery. Skill Books Shop sells Common (Military/Development/Subordinate City) only Lv1-3, Duty only Lv1; higher levels obtained elsewhere. Duty officer (Lv20) increases Research speed; ideal Sun Wu, Hammurabi, or Eulji Mundeok (any general works for base buff). Research speed buffs stack: Typography/Super Typography research (max +70%/+100%), Intellect Crown gear, Academy duty officer, Military Academy duty officer (also boosts Academy research, Keep 36), Code of Hammurabi treasure, American subordinate city, Alliance Science.

title: Keep: Upgrade Requirements, etc
summary: Keep is the master gate for all building upgrades; Lv16 unlocks duty officer for construction speed/training capacity.
content: Keep is the starting point/gate for construction — nearly every other building's upgrade requires a minimum Keep level. Keep level shown on map (e.g. k25, k40). Functions: exchange gems for gold (Levy), Champion (heroine interactions for rewards/buffs), City Buff items, Duty Officer assignment, Change Culture, Change City Decoration. Raising Keep level increases: max generals held, number of free levies, gold per levy, resource gathering speed, in-city resource production speed; also unlocks other buildings/features per level. To raise Keep level, Walls and other facility levels must be raised first (gating requirement chain). Max Lv50 seen in data (requires Walls 49, Archer Camp 48, Workshop 49, etc. at high levels; construction speed buff scales to +600% by Lv50). Duty Officer unlocked at Keep Lv16: increases Construction Speed and Training Capacity; ideal officers Hera, Ashoka, Zhuge Liang, or Menshikov (any general works for base buff).

title: Construction / City Development Guide
summary: Priority Academy & Rally Spot first; buffs from research/gear/duty officer speed construction; several buildings disable core function while upgrading.
content: Beginner priority: after raising Keep level, upgrade Academy and Rally Spot first — Academy research raises many buffs, Rally Spot increases march size for gathering/combat. Construction speed boosted by: Academy Construction/Adv Construction/Super Construction research plus Typography/Super Typography to speed that research; Strength Crown gear before starting; Duty Officer at the Keep before starting (ideal Menshikov or Zhuge Liang, +20% base skill); Alliance Science; also Civilization Treasure (Mask of Agamemnon), Victory Column, Dragon (Celtic Demon). Buildings that disable core function during their OWN upgrade: Hospital (no healing, SvS revive ok), Shrine (no general revive), Walls (no fire-extinguish/repair until done, then full recovery), Academy & Military Academy (no research), Forge (no crafting, but Dragon->Ares upgrade at Lv30 still works), Stables/Archer Camp/Barracks/Workshop (no troop training), Research Factory (no research-stone production), Bunker (no troop deposit). Consuming Return Event matters most at Keep level 25+.

title: Embassy
summary: Embassy gates War Hall upgrade and raises reinforcement capacity, help-count/speed, and resource-transport limits; duty slot Lv27.
content: Embassy lets you view/manage reinforcements sent to/from alliance members (send back/recall). Raising Embassy level increases: reinforcement receiving capacity; number of construction-help received and shortens help time; resource-transport amount, number of transports, receivable amount, and lowers transport fee rate. Construction possible at Keep Lv9; requires Market to be raised first; raising Embassy level is itself a prerequisite to raise War Hall level. All menus remain usable during Embassy's own upgrade (nothing disabled). Duty Officer slot unlocked at Embassy Lv27: provides buffs to Reinforcing Troop Attack, Defense, and HP; ideal officers Queen Anne or Heraclius (any general works for base buff). Max Lv50 (Keep 50 + Market 50 required at top level).

title: Barracks
summary: Trains Ground troops; cannot train while upgrading; duty slot at Lv25 boosts training speed & capacity.
content: Barracks trains Ground troops (cannot create soldiers while Barracks itself is upgrading, per Construction Guide). Gated by Keep level (Keep 1 at Lv1, up to Keep 50 + Triumphal Arch 2 at Lv48-49; Lv50 requires Keep 50 + Triumphal Arch 2 and unlocks Tier 17 Troop). Duty Officer unlocked at Barracks Lv25: increases Ground Troop Training Speed & Training Capacity; ideal officers Abd al-Rahman I, Liu Bei, or Jiang Ziya (any general works for base buff).

title: Archer Camp
summary: Trains Ranged troops; cannot train while upgrading; duty slot at Lv21 boosts training speed & capacity.
content: Archer Camp trains Ranged troops (cannot create soldiers while upgrading, per Construction Guide). Gated by Keep level (Keep 1 at Lv1, requires Keep 48 + Archer Tower 48 + Barracks 48 around Lv48; Lv50 requires Keep 50 + Archer Tower 50 and unlocks Tier 17 Troop). Duty Officer unlocked at Archer Camp Lv21: increases Ranged Troop Training Speed & Training Capacity; ideal officers John Hawkwood, Sulla, or John I of Portugal (any general works for base buff).

title: Stables
summary: Trains Mounted troops; cannot train while upgrading; duty slot at Lv33 boosts training speed & capacity.
content: Stables trains Mounted troops (cannot create soldiers while upgrading, per Construction Guide). Gated by Keep level starting at Keep 6 for Lv1-3; Lv50 requires Keep 50 and unlocks Tier 17 Troop; Lv49 additionally requires Archer Camp 49. Duty Officer unlocked at Stables Lv33: increases Mounted Troop Training Speed & Training Capacity; ideal officers Oleg of Novgorod, John Adams, or Stilicho (any general works for base buff).

title: Workshop
summary: Trains Siege machines; cannot train while upgrading; duty slot at Lv29 boosts training speed & capacity.
content: Workshop trains Siege machines (cannot create soldiers while upgrading, per Construction Guide). Gated by Keep + Sawmill level early (Keep 6 Sawmill 1 at Lv1); later requires War Hall level (Keep 48 War Hall 48 at Lv48); Lv49 requires War Hall 49 + Stables 49; Lv50 requires Keep 50 + War Hall 50 and unlocks Tier 17 Troop. Duty Officer unlocked at Workshop Lv29: increases Siege Machine Training Speed & Training Capacity; ideal officers Gwon Ryul, Mikhail Kutuzov, or Roger II (any general works for base buff).

title: Army Camp
summary: Upgrade-requirement building gated by Keep level; no duty officer slot documented; scales to Keep 50.
content: Army Camp upgrade requirements scale purely with Keep level (Keep 6 required from Lv1 through Lv50; no other building prerequisites listed, unlike most buildings which also need sibling-building levels). Lv1 costs only 520 Food/520 Lumber, 00:00:10 build time, power 5 — a cheap early target relative to other Lv1 buildings (which typically need 1,000/1,000). Costs and construction time scale up to Lv50 (Keep 50 requirement, +610% speed buff shown at max, power 48,000,000, ~8,193d build time unbuffed). No duty officer section or milestone unlocks documented in this source for Army Camp itself.

title: Military Academy
summary: Unlocked at Keep 36 (required to reach Keep 37); battle-focused research using Tactic Scroll+Gold not Research Stone; duty officer also speeds Academy research.
content: Military Academy enables research not available at the main Academy, mainly battle-oriented buffs/debuffs; higher Military Academy level unlocks more research tiers. Uses Tactic Scroll + Gold (no Research Stone). Construction possible at Keep Lv36; Military Academy must reach Lv1 before Keep can reach Lv37 (hard gate). Max level 15 (research cost jumps sharply at level 5, cheaper from level 6 on — advice: keep level-5 research minimal, rush to level 6). Military Research cannot be started while Military Academy itself is upgrading (menu selectable, can't start new). Duty Officer unlocked once Military Academy reaches Lv1: increases research speed at BOTH Military Academy and the main Academy; ideal officers Sargon or Justinian (any general works for base buff). Tip: set duty officers at both Academy and Military Academy simultaneously to stack research speed.

title: Research Factory
summary: Produces Materials, Research Stones, and crafts Historic Medals; gated by Keep 21 + Hospital; duty slot Lv32.
content: Research Factory produces Materials (Forge/Wonder crafting), Research Stones (higher-level Academy research), and crafts Historic Medals (Council of State promotions). Construction possible at Keep Lv21; requires Hospital raised first. Cannot produce Research Stones while Research Factory itself is upgrading. Milestones: Lv21 Lv3-material production, Lv32 Duty Officer, Lv36 Lv4 materials, Lv41 Lv5 materials. Material production free but slow (2+ hrs/item), up to 8 queued; gem-rush expensive, not recommended. Research Stone production optional/late-game (rarely needed before Keep ~33); costs gold+gems (100pc=500k gold/8k gems up to 1500pc=7.5M gold/120k gems, no bulk discount). Daily production limit raised by: factory quests, Research Factory level, duty officer. Production SPEED raised only by duty officer (Academy/Monarch Gear research speed-ups do NOT affect Research Stone production). Duty Officer at Lv32: raises Research Stone production limit & speed; ideal Margaret of Valois, then Stephen the Great (any general for base buff).

title: Arsenal
summary: Upgrades/refines already-created troops; buildable at Keep 27; capped at Lv1 (no further levels).
content: Arsenal is where already-created soldiers get upgraded (troop tier/attribute refinement) — see Troop Upgrade Guide for details on what to prioritize. Buildable at Keep Lv27; Lv1 costs 58,000,000 Food, 74,000,000 Lumber, 76,000,000 Stone, 74,000,000 Ore and takes 75d 21:19 at 0% speed buff. Unlike most buildings, Arsenal is capped at Lv1 — no further upgrades exist beyond level 1, so its one-time build cost is the entire investment required. No duty-officer slot documented for Arsenal.

title: Hospital
summary: Heals wounded troops & revives SvS dead; cannot heal while upgrading; duty slot Lv15 raises capacity & heal speed.
content: Hospital heals wounded soldiers and revives soldiers who died during SvS. Does NOT handle: siege machines (Trap Factory repairs), soldier souls (Holy Palace revives), deserters (Holy Palace recalls), dead generals (Shrine revives). Construction possible at Keep Lv6; Research Factory upgrade requires Hospital raised first. Healing disabled while Hospital itself is upgrading (SvS Revive still usable). Duty Officer unlocked at Hospital Lv15: increases Hospital Capacity & Healing Speed; ideal Blanche, then Pericles/Skanderbeg (any general for base buff). Hospital capacity also raised by: Academy research (Hospital Scale +50,000 max, Super Hospital Scale +150% max, Supreme Hospital Scale +50,000 max; Adv Medical Power +100%), Military Academy Ground Troop Formation research, defense generals at red star 2+ (Richard the Lionheart even at star 0 gives +30%), Dragon of Thebes on a defender (Lv10 +25%, Lv15 +35%), Monarch Talent Lv30 'Hospital Capacity' (+25%), SvS temporarily +50%. In special battlefields (Gaugamela, Constantinople) capacity is unlimited so these buffs don't matter there.

title: Warehouse
summary: Protects resources from looting/upkeep and raises storage limit; gates Pasture upgrade; duty slot Lv23.
content: Warehouse raises: protection of in-city resources from enemy looting when attacked/defeated, protected food amount exempt from automatic troop-upkeep consumption, and storage limit for gathered/relic resources. Pasture upgrade requires Warehouse level raised first. All menus usable during Warehouse's own upgrade. Duty Officer unlocked at Warehouse Lv23: increases protection amount from looting and upkeep; ideal Philippa, then Guinevere (any general for base buff), tiers: Junior +20%, Medium +40%, Senior +60%, Special +100%, Super +150%. 5 ways to raise upkeep-exempt food: raise Warehouse level; Academy research Adv Silo (Advancement category, max +200%); assign defense general Shajar al-Durr (+200%); Warehouse duty officer (up to +150% Super); Monarch Talent Lv30 'Troop Upkeep' (+100%). Example scaling: Warehouse Lv26 -> ~17.6M with partial buffs; Lv30 -> ~160M with Shajar+Medium officer+research lv20+Monarch Talent (540% total buff); Lv35 -> ~525M with Shajar+Super officer+research lv20+Monarch Talent (600% total).

title: Walls
summary: Defensive structure gating Keep level; cannot extinguish fire/repair defense damage while upgrading until complete.
content: Walls upgrade requirement scales with Keep level only (Keep 1 at Lv1 up to Keep 50 at Lv50, matching Keep-level gate for each tier). Per Construction Guide: cannot extinguish fire or repair damage from a lost defense while Walls itself is upgrading — but completing the upgrade recovers all accumulated damage at once. Walls level is a hard prerequisite for raising Keep level (Keep progression requires Walls kept in step, e.g. Keep Lv3 requires Walls Lv2).

title: Watchtower
summary: Provides scouting/incoming-march intel and gates Forge Dragon/Ares/Achaemenidae equipment tiers; full features at Lv41.
content: Watchtower gives scouting reports and warns of incoming marches; all features fully unlocked at Watchtower Lv41. Forge-related gates: Lv27 required for Dragon equipment production (Forge Lv27), Lv30 for Ares equipment (Forge Lv30), Lv33 for Achaemenidae equipment (Forge Lv33) — i.e. raising Forge past these tiers requires matching Watchtower level. Scout-info milestones: Lv4 city resource amounts, Lv8 incoming troop count, Lv11 target's subordinate city count, Lv14 target's defending troop count, Lv17 target's Wall HP/reinforcement amount/Archer Tower level/trap types & levels, Lv19 Truce Agreement Informant usable, Lv21 incoming troop type, Lv25 target's defending troop type, Lv27 target's reinforcement type, Lv30 target's troop buffs, Lv37 target's Main City Defense Generals, Lv41 target's reinforcement buffs. Gated by Keep+Farm level (Keep 6 Farm 1 at Lv1, scaling to Keep 50 Farm 50 at Lv50).

title: Market
summary: Provides tax collection, black market, auctions; gates Embassy upgrade; Black Market at Lv11, duty slot Lv24.
content: Market provides: Tax (free resource collection limited times/day, gems for extra, resets daily), Black Market (unlocked Lv11, better products at higher level), Auction House (event-period only). Construction possible at Keep Lv6; Embassy upgrade requires Market raised first. All menus usable during Market's own upgrade. Milestones: Lv11 unlocks Black Market, Lv24 unlocks Duty Officer. Duty Officer unlocked at Market Lv24: increases Resource Tax Amount; ideal Yolande of Aragon, then Michael VIII (any general for base buff).

title: Tavern
summary: Source of generals via refresh/purchase; buildable from Keep 1; Lv3 unlocks Wheel of Fortune.
content: Tavern is the primary source of recruitable generals (refreshable roster, chance at historic/golden historic generals via refresh — see Drop Rate of Historic General at Tavern for refresh-count odds). Buildable starting Keep Lv1 (Lv1 has no resource cost). Lv3 unlocks Wheel of Fortune and requires Keep 3. Scales up to Lv50 (Keep 50 requirement, power 62,000,000 at max, +610% speed buff at top tier). No duty officer slot documented for Tavern in this source.

title: Wonder
summary: Unlocks Dragons (via Dragon Cliff), crafts Civilization Equipment/Treasure; upgradeable from Keep 30; only 3 levels, each gated by Glory achievements.
content: Wonder building is present from the start with all menus available (Glory achievements view, Civilization Equipment crafting, Civilization Treasure unlock/upgrade/equip, Advanced Refining once all 4 refining attributes reach Gold). Wonder can be upgraded once Keep reaches Lv30; only 3 levels total, each requiring a specific Glory achievement plus Badges/Research Stones: Lv1 needs Server War Ares glory (SvS Top5) + 1,700 Badges + 4,000 Research Stones, unlocks Dragon Norway Ridge; Lv2 needs Monarch's Glory (be King/Conqueror twice) + 2,800 Badges + 6,600 Research Stones, unlocks Dragon of Thebes; Lv3 needs Invincible Hero glory (Constantinople Top200) + 4,600 Badges + 10,800 Research Stones, unlocks Nidhogg. Civilization Equipment crafting does NOT require raising Wonder level, but does require Keep Lv33. Crafting speed buffs: Academy 'Blacksmith' research (Defense category, requires Academy Lv14) + Typography/Super Typography to speed that research; Agile Crown monarch gear; Forge duty officer (any general).

title: Art Hall
summary: General Hall & Exhibition Hall rewards from collecting generals/artwork; buildable Keep 11; gates Keep 28/33 and Triumphal Arch.
content: Art Hall grants rewards/buffs for collecting Generals (General Hall) and Artwork (Exhibition Hall); also manages Art Treasure Buffs and Civilization Cards. Construction possible at Keep Lv11. Raising Keep to Lv28 or Lv33, and raising Triumphal Arch, all require Art Hall level raised first (hard gate). All menus usable during Art Hall's own upgrade; no locked menus at all — fully available from start. Milestones: Lv11-25 unlock successive Exhibition Hall reward tiers; Lv12 and Lv27-35 unlock successive General Hall reward tiers. Scales to Lv50 (Keep 50 requirement at max).

title: Shrine
summary: Revives dead generals; cannot revive while upgrading (per Construction guide); Lv27+ grants temporary King's Protection combat buff via offerings.
content: Shrine's core function (implied, detailed in Construction Guide) is reviving dead generals — disabled while Shrine itself is upgrading. Starting at Shrine Lv27, a 'King's Protection' temporary buff (24h duration) becomes available, activated after a required number of offerings: Lv27 = Ranged Attack +10% (14 offers); Lv30 = Ranged Attack + Mounted Defense +10% (14 offers); Lv33 = Ranged Attack + Mounted&Ground Defense +15% (14 offers); scaling up to Lv42 = Ranged&Siege Attack + Mounted&Ground Defense +35% (29 offers); Lv45 = same categories +40% (34 offers); Lv48 = same categories +45% (39 offers, max shown). Buildable from Keep Lv1 (Shrine Lv1 has no resource cost).

title: Bunker
summary: Shelters troops from combat up to 12h; not usable while upgrading; duty slot Lv20; max protection capacity 800,000 at Lv40+Super officer.
content: Bunker protects deposited soldiers from combat for up to 12 hours; higher Bunker level and a duty officer increase how many troops can be deposited. Not available for deposits while Bunker itself is upgrading. Construction possible at Keep Lv9. Duty Officer unlocked at Bunker Lv20: increases Bunker protecting capacity; ideal Caterina Sforza or Clovis I (any general for base buff). Capacity scaling: base capacity rises with level (100,000 at Lv20 up to 400,000 at Lv40); officer tiers add flat amounts: Junior +25,000, Medium +50,000, Senior +75,000, Special +100,000, Super +400,000. Maximum possible protection capacity is 800,000, achieved at Bunker Lv40 + Super Officer.

title: Trap Factory
summary: Builds wall-defense traps (Trap/Rock/Abatis/Fire Arrow, each countering a troop type); gated by Keep+Sawmill; duty slot Lv19.
content: Trap Factory manufactures 4 trap types with distinct counter roles: Trap = anti-Ground (260% vs ground), Rock = anti-Ranged, Abatis = anti-Mounted, Fire Arrow = anti-Siege. Trap tiers unlock progressively by Trap Factory level (e.g. Lv1 Trap I, Lv2 Rock I, Lv3 Abatis I, Lv4 Fire Arrow I ... up to Lv46-50 unlocking tier X of each). Gated by Keep + Sawmill level (Keep 8 Sawmill 1 at Lv1, scaling to Keep 50 at Lv50). Duty Officer unlocked at Trap Factory Lv19: increases Trap Building Speed & Damaged Siege Machine Capacity (repair capacity for siege machines); ideal Tachibana Dosetsu, Otto the Great, or Askia Muhammad I (any general for base buff).

title: Pasture
summary: Levels up Spiritual Beasts, Dragons, and Seals; gated by Keep 21 + Warehouse; gates Council of State tiers alongside Victory Column.
content: Pasture is where Spiritual Beasts, Dragons, and Seals are leveled/refined. Construction possible at Keep Lv21; requires Warehouse raised first. Council of State level-ups require both Pasture and Victory Column raised first. All menus usable during Pasture's own upgrade. Spiritual Beast unlock milestones: Lv6 Thunder Scorpion, Lv11 Bird of Hurricane, Lv16 Lord of Lava, Lv22 Behemoth King. Dragon unlock milestones: Lv26 unlocks Dragon feature plus Celtic Demon (needs 300,000 gems) and Jawzahr (needs Jawzahr Dragon Pattern); Lv30 unlocks Fafnir (needs 1,400 Dragon Crystals); Lv35 unlocks each dragon's 3rd Talent (requires that dragon at Lv10). Council of State gating: Pasture Lv27→CoS Lv2 start requirement, Lv28→CoS Lv3, Lv29→CoS Lv4, Lv30→CoS Lv5, Lv31→CoS Lv6, Lv32→CoS Lv7-9, Lv33→CoS Lv10-12, Lv34→CoS Lv13-15, Lv35→CoS Lv16-45 (i.e. Pasture Lv35 is needed for all remaining Council of State levels).

title: Farm
summary: Farm building; upgrade costs (lumber/stone/ore/food) and power scale hugely across 50 levels, gated by matching Keep level.
content: Farm is the resource building tied to food production; each level requires a matching Keep level (Lv1 needs Keep 1, gating continues up to Keep 50 at Lv50). Upgrade costs rise from a few hundred Lumber at Lv1 to billions of Lumber/Stone/Ore at Lv48-50 (e.g. Lv50: 8,009,000,000 Lumber, 7,973,000,000 Stone, 9,082,000,000 Ore, 0 Food). Construction speed bonus needed/implied rises to +610% by Lv50, and construction time balloons to 5,338 days 17:53 at Lv50 (before speedups). Power granted per level rises from 2 (Lv1) to 20,000,000 (Lv50). No explicit production-rate or storage-capacity numbers are given on this page for the Farm itself; only the building's own upgrade cost/power/time table is documented.

title: Mine
summary: Mine building (ore resource) upgrade requirements; requires Keep 11 minimum; costs and power scale to Lv50.
content: Mine is the resource building associated with ore. Unlike Farm/Sawmill (buildable from Keep 1), Mine requires Keep level 11 as its minimum requirement even at Lv1, and remains gated at Keep 11 through many levels before the gate rises with level (e.g. Lv48 needs Keep 48, Lv50 needs Keep 50). Upgrade costs use Food+Lumber (not Stone/Ore) for the Mine's own construction, scaling from 260/260 at Lv1 to 5,972,000,000 Food / 8,009,000,000 Lumber / 7,973,000,000 Stone at Lv50. Construction speed bonus scales to +610% and time to 5,338d 17:53 by Lv50. Power granted scales from 3 (Lv1) to 20,000,000 (Lv50). No explicit ore-production-rate or storage-cap numbers are stated on this page; only the building's own construction cost/power table is documented.

title: Quarry
summary: Quarry building (stone resource) upgrade requirements; requires Keep 6 minimum; costs and power scale to Lv50.
content: Quarry is the resource building associated with stone. It requires Keep level 6 as its minimum requirement at Lv1 (higher gate than Farm/Sawmill's Keep 1, lower than Mine's Keep 11), with the Keep requirement rising alongside level at higher tiers (Keep 48 for Lv48, etc.). Upgrade costs use Food+Lumber for the Quarry's own construction, scaling from 260/260 at Lv1 to 5,972,000,000 Food / 8,009,000,000 Lumber / 9,082,000,000 Ore by Lv50. Construction speed bonus scales to +610% and construction time to 5,338d 17:53 at Lv50. Power granted scales from 3 (Lv1) to 20,000,000 (Lv50). No explicit stone-production-rate or storage-cap numbers are given on this page; only the building's own upgrade cost/power table is documented.

title: Sawmill
summary: Sawmill produces lumber; higher level = faster production + larger temp storage (caps in 12h, must collect manually); gates other Lv21 buildings.
content: Sawmill is the resource building that produces lumber. Increasing Sawmill level increases both the speed of lumber production and the maximum amount of temporary in-building storage. Once production begins, the storage cap is reached in 12 hours, so lumber must be collected manually before then (via the wood icon on the Sawmill or the arm icon on the Warehouse) or production effectively stalls. Sawmill must be leveled up before Archer Camp, Workshop, Trap Factory, and Keep can be upgraded to level 21 (Sawmill is a prerequisite gate for those Lv21 upgrades). Buildable from Keep 1 (Lv1 requires only Keep 1, no Lumber/Stone/Ore cost, only 260 Food). Upgrade costs scale to billions of Food/Stone/Ore by Lv48-50 (e.g. Lv50: 5,972,000,000 Food, 7,973,000,000 Stone, 9,082,000,000 Ore); construction speed bonus reaches +610% and time reaches 5,338d 17:53 at Lv50. Power granted scales from 2 (Lv1) to 20,000,000 (Lv50).

title: Troop Type Guide: Usage & Characteristics (Mounted, Ground, Ranged, Siege)
summary: 4 troop types with rock-paper-scissors counters, roles (PvE/PvP/gathering), stat pros/cons, siege range-by-tier, and combat formula/coefficients.
content: 4 troop types: Mounted, Ground, Ranged, Siege. Basic roles: Mounted=PvE monster hunting + PvP vs Ground (highest attack/speed); Ground=PvP vs Ranged + resource gathering (2nd highest Load, high Defense/HP); Ranged=PvP vs Mounted (best Range, weak Speed/Defense/HP); Siege=PvP vs Ranged + resource gathering (highest Load, longest range, weakest Attack/Defense/HP). Rock-paper-scissors at equal tier/buff: Mounted beats Ground, Ground beats Ranged, Ranged beats Mounted; Siege beats Ranged but loses to Mounted/Ground, and is 'extremely' strong (kill rates 10-23x) vs any lower-tier troops. Close-combat types (Mounted, Ground) beat long-range types (Ranged, Siege) if they can close the distance. Siege range increased in a 2020 update by tier: T1-4=1,400, T5-8=1,556, T9-10=1,711, T11-12=1,867, T13-14=2,178 (ranged troop range is 500). Combat formula (per site admin comment): Number Defeated = Attack x Number of Troops x Troop Compatibility Coefficient x (Attack/(Attack+Enemy Defense)) / Enemy HP. Troop compatibility coefficients (attacker:defender): Ground vs Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege = 1/1.2/0.7/1.1; Ranged vs same = 0.8/1/1.2/1.1; Mounted vs same = 1.2/0.8/1/0.9; Siege vs same = 0.35/0.4/0.3/0.5. Power (a rough strength estimate) is identical across all 4 troop types at the same tier despite differing Attack/Defense/HP splits. T12 troops are widely believed unusually strong because the game's starting 'City of Throne' troops were coded as T12.

title: Troop Upgrade Guide (Arsenal)
summary: Arsenal (Keep 27) upgrades troops T10->T14 one tier at a time; costs resources+time; upgrading costs more than building new but converts existing troops.
content: The Arsenal building (requires Keep 27) lets players upgrade existing troops one tier at a time: T10->11->12->13->14; troops below T9 cannot be upgraded at all. Requirements: the destination-tier facility (Barracks/Archer Camp/Stables/Workshop for Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege respectively) must already be built to the target level; troops must be inside the city (not gathering, hunting, rallying, or ghosting) and not wounded/in hospital; upgrades take time (speed-up-able with gems) and temporarily reduce monarch power by the upgraded troops' value until completion. Upgrading is NOT counted as 'Troop Training' for activity/event score purposes (except if completed on a 'Power Increase' event day). Economically, building a new T(n+1) troop directly always costs less total resources than building T(n) then upgrading it to T(n+1), though the upgrade step alone costs less than building new. Strategic guidance: avoid mass-building troops below T9 (wastes resources/speedups); aim to start serious training at T10 (Keep 25) or ideally T12 (Keep 30); once at Keep 33+, stop trying to fully ghost troops and instead build large defensive numbers, targeting roughly T13: 500k->1M, T12: 1M->1.5M, T11-and-below: 100k->500k per type over time (~2M+ total troops as a baseline to withstand Keep 35 attacks, buff/debuff-dependent). Upgrading Mounted troops preferentially to T12/T13 is recommended early for monster hunting (e.g. needed to beat Witch 3/4, Warlord 3/4) since Mounted is less useful for city defense.

title: Troops Initial Stats List
summary: Base Attack/Defense/HP/Speed/Range/Power/Load/Upkeep per troop type across tiers T1-T17 (T17 added Oct 2025); all stats rise sharply with tier.
content: Base stats (pre-buff) for Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege across tiers T1-T17 (T17 added 10/23/2025). T1: Ground atk100/def300/hp600; Ranged atk130/def100/hp250; Mounted atk220/def150/hp400; Siege atk100/def50/hp100. T17 (max): Ground atk5,660/def15,720/hp32,480; Ranged atk6,280/def5,050/hp12,340; Mounted atk10,100/def6,650/hp18,230; Siege atk5,060/def2,390/hp5,060 (~50-56x T1 values). Speed is constant across tiers per type except Siege: Ground350, Ranged100, Mounted displayed as 600 but internal DB value is 300, Siege75(T1-15)/76(T16-17). Range constant except Siege: Ground50, Ranged500, Mounted50; Siege displayed/internal-DB values: T1-4=1400/900, T5-8=1556/1000, T9-10=1711/1100, T11-12=1867/1200, T13-17=2178/1400. Power (same for all troop types per tier) rises from 2 (T1) to 163(T15), 230(T16), 310(T17) -- roughly 155x T1-to-T17. Load ranking Siege>Ground>Ranged=Mounted; T1: Ground10/Ranged8/Mounted8/Siege14 -> T17: Ground70/Ranged60/Mounted60/Siege100. Upkeep (food/time) ranking Mounted>Ground>Ranged=Siege; T1: Ground0.2/Ranged0.16/Mounted0.24/Siege0.2 -> T17: Ground16.5/Ranged15/Mounted19.8/Siege15, roughly an 80x increase in food upkeep from T1 to T17 -- notably less than the ~155x power increase, meaning higher-tier troops give more power per unit of food upkeep despite far higher absolute upkeep.

title: Dead City Power List
summary: Rough monarch-power thresholds by Keep level to identify a 'dead' (abandoned/no-troop) city before attacking, plus gray zones and alive-city comparisons.
content: Reference thresholds (rough) for identifying a dead/abandoned city by Keep level and monarch power, with a 'gray zone' where it's ambiguous: Keep28 dead if <=16M power (gray zone 16M-22M); Keep26 <=10M (gray 10M-14M); Keep24 <=5M (gray 5M-10M); Keep20 <=1.5M (gray 1.5M-2.5M); Keep16 <=500K (gray 500K-2M); Keep12 <=100K (gray 100K-200K). Power correlates with monarch level as well as Keep level, so a low-Keep city with a high monarch level (long playtime, more research/construction) can show deceptively high power while still being effectively dead -- Keep level alone cannot reliably classify power in the gray zone. Reference survey data shows huge variance among 'alive' cities at the same Keep level depending on investment: e.g. Keep35 alive cities ranged from 338M (monarch lvl33) up to 6.9B (monarch lvl35) in the sample.

title: Ideal Land
summary: Ideal Land is a Keep-12 garden-ornament building granting Construction Speed and Troop HP/Attack/Defense buffs, leveled via Prosperity Points from placed Ornaments.
content: Note: despite its name, 'Ideal Land' is not about choosing city/gathering tiles -- it is a building (unlocked at Keep 12) featuring a garden where players place 'Ornaments' to earn buffs and Prosperity Points; upgrading Ideal Land itself also grants buffs, and Ideal Land Lv7+ is required to upgrade the Triumphal Arch beyond Lv5. Ideal Land Lv1-10 buffs: Construction Speed +2% (Lv1) up to +47% (Lv10); All Troops HP/Attack/Defense each +5% (Lv2) up to +45% (Lv10). Three ornament types: Basic (no buff, no points -- skip); Gem-purchasable (no buff, but placing the full set gives +450 Prosperity Points for 460,000 gems total); Limited (both buffs AND points, only obtainable via special/anniversary events like 'Voyage to Civilizations' using Golden Commemorative Coins/Golden Ladders, not gem-purchasable). Limited ornaments level 1-10 via collecting duplicates (cost rises from 1 duplicate for Lv1->2 up to 6 for Lv9->10); focusing upgrades on one ornament yields more meaningful buffs per ornament, while spreading across many ornaments yields more total Prosperity Points per resource spent. Example max-level (Lv10) Limited ornament buffs: Notre-Dame de Paris / Altar of the Fatherland grant March Size Capacity +5,000 plus ~15% attack buffs and ~10% enemy-defense debuffs. Garden space can be expanded with gems: 32x32 costs 50,000 gems (needs Ideal Land Lv3), 40x40 costs 100,000 gems (needs Ideal Land Lv5); 40x40 is the max size.

title: Art Treasure Guide (Buff list, Upgrade requirement, etc)
summary: 16 Art Treasures give permanent troop buffs/debuffs, unlocked via Exhibition Hall/General Hall set completion, leveled 1-8 with Scrolls + Artwork Fragments.
content: Art Treasures are permanent buffs unlocked in the Art Hall by completing collection sets in the Exhibition Hall and General Hall; each of the 16 named treasures (Excalibur, Mona Lisa, Great Imperial Crown, Discobolus, Terracotta Army, Tutankhamun's Mask, Ring of Fafnir, Champion Knight Armor, Nike of Samothrace, Celtic Chariot, Golden Eagle Flag, Trojan Horse, Laurel Wreath, Yasakani no Magatama, Iron Crown, Gold Cup of Eternal Stability) can be leveled Lv1-8 for a stronger buff. Example: Excalibur gives Attacking Ground/Ranged/Mounted Troop Attack +5% (Lv1) scaling to +85% (Lv8); Gold Cup of Eternal Stability gives, when attacking, enemy Ranged Troop Defense -10%->-185%, when defending enemy Ground Troop Attack -10%->-185%, plus own Ground Troop Attack +5%->+55%, all scaling Lv1->Lv8. Leveling up a treasure requires a treasure-specific 'Art Treasure Scroll' plus 4 types of 'Artwork Fragment' (also treasure-specific, e.g. Knight's Sword/Leg Armor/Armor/Helmet for Excalibur); material costs per level jump rise from 40-80 units (Lv1->2) to 1,300-4,180 units (Lv7->8). Activation of each treasure requires unlocking specific Exhibition Hall categories (e.g. Excalibur needs Knight Equipment Hall, Stringed Instrument Hall, Jewelry Hall) or General Hall tiers (e.g. Gold Cup of Eternal Stability needs Era of Glory, Tactical Grandmaster, Eternal Renown general-collection tiers).

title: How to get Resources Efficiently (Items, Gathering, Production)
summary: Resource items mainly drop from high-value boss kills and Consuming Return Events; ranked by stamina efficiency and RSS-spend refund thresholds.
content: Resource chests mostly come from killing high-value bosses and from Consuming Return Events (not from gathering, which is covered elsewhere); ranked by stamina efficiency: Azazel 400K/stamina, Kraken 320K/stamina, Ymir6 290K/stamina, Ymir3 266K/stamina, Ymir5 260K/stamina, Stymphalian Bird/Ymir4 240K/stamina, Witch6/Warlord6 210K/stamina, Nian5/Royal Thief/Ammit 200K/stamina; by raw yield per kill, Azazel/Kraken/Stymphalian Bird/Ymir give 2.4M-5M x4 RSS types. Resource Consuming Return Event (f2p if RSS is farmed/gathered): spend 2.5B of one type (~Keep31) for 360M+ back cumulative; 5B (~K33) -> 800M+; 10B (~K35) -> 1.6B+; 20B -> 3B+; 35B -> 4.8B+, each per type. Speedup Consuming Return Event: spending 250,000 speedup-minutes (~173 days, cumulative) also returns 60M x4 RSS types.

title: How to get Gold efficiently
summary: Ranked gold sources: BoC/BoG battles, Crazy Eggs, double-drop Goblin/Bayard/Viking kills, World Boss, Trade Products, Tavern Activity, login rewards, gem-to-gold Levy.
content: Free gold sources ranked: Battle of Constantinople (BoC, top-20 alliance only) guarantees 2M+ gold win or lose, up to 60M with a high score; Battle of Gaugamela (BoG) guarantees 2.4M+, up to 55M; Crazy Eggs event gives 4.4M+/day (up to 1.2M per lucky 8-hammer egg smash plus a 1.6M activation bonus); killing Goblins with a Double-Drop general (Baibars/Cleopatra + King's Ring, 46%+ rate) yields up to 4M gold per Lv7 Golden Goblin; Knight Bayard kills with double-drop give 200K-2M gold per kill; Viking event gives 2.5-3.5M gold for 50 Easy Vikings, 400M+ possible via alliance rally on Hard; World Boss gives 250K-1.5M gold (5 free fights/day) plus 250K-5M for top-100 damage rank; Trade Products event gives 2M gold (50 exchange items needed); Tavern Activity score gives ~1.9-2.5M gold/week at Keep 28-33; a 10-day login event gave 6.2M total. Non-free: convert gems to gold via maxed Coinage research + Levy at Keep (e.g. 5,000 gems -> 13.5M gold at Keep28), but the exchange rate worsens sharply past 15,000 gems/day and resets daily.

title: How to get Gems
summary: F2P gem sources ranked by weekly yield: World Boss, Treasure Hunt, Arctic Barbarians, Crazy Eggs, Lucky Apple, Server War, shrine offerings, Server Gifts.
content: F2P gem sources ranked by yield: World Boss up to 300K gems/week (10 fights/day, first 5 free, up to 5,000/fight by damage, +4K-10K for top-100 personal rank); Treasure Hunt (Pyramid) event up to 189,000/event at 500M score (+up to 300,000 for top monarch rank); Northern Invaders (Arctic Barbarians) up to 510,000/event at 2B score; Crazy Eggs 5,600-75,000/week during the ~11-day event; Dwarf's Lucky Apple gives 15,000 per lucky Witch's Gift; Server War (biweekly) 2,000-84,500 by monarch score (+50,000-300,000 for top-10); Monarch Competition 3,500+/week (5 stages); BoC ~8,000+/week and BoG ~1,000+/week (top-20 alliance only, guaranteed win or lose); Server Gifts on Wall ~700+/week (50 gems at 11:00/23:00 server time, chance of 100); daily shrine offerings 2,500/week (7x/week); tavern Activity score 350-1,000+/week; King's Path (stamina spend) 100-2,800; Undead Invasion ~500+/week. Note: gathering gems from gem-mine resource tiles is explicitly NOT recommended - very slow versus these methods and it blocks resource gathering/monster hunting time.

title: How to get Speedup items efficiently
summary: Speedups mainly come from Consuming Return Events and boss kills (Cerberus/Pan best); BoC gives 50-220h guaranteed; two chest types drop random speedups.
content: F2P speedup sources ranked: Consuming Return Event gives the largest cumulative totals - completing 2.5B RSS of all 4 types returns 640+ days of speedups, 10B -> 3,100+ days, 35B -> 9,500+ days; consuming gems also returns Training/Construction speedups (up to 1,883 days Training + 837 days Construction total at 8M gems spent); Battle of Constantinople gives 50-220 hours (top-20 alliance only, guaranteed even on a loss). Killing bosses is the main daily source - Cerberus and Pan are rated the best, then Ymir, then Griffin/Witch3/Warlord3/Hydra3/Phoenix (best farmed via a high-rank alliance's active rally); Legendary Cerberus Lv4's chest alone gives 248h combined across Construction/Training/Healing/Research/Trap/Craft speedups. All bosses from Lv1 Zombie (0.5h) up to Senior Cerberus Lv3 (239h) drop speedups 100% of the time. Random bonus chests: Royal Chest (from defeating 3 Royal Thief) gives an 8h/24h/3-day/7-day speedup; Viking Rescue Chest (post-Viking Trial) gives a 3h/8h/3-day Healing speedup.

title: How to get Hammer efficiently
summary: Hammers smash Crazy Eggs (60-70+ needed/cycle); best F2P source is farming low-level bosses with a double-drop general, plus gathering Lv6+ resource tiles.
content: Hammers are needed to smash Crazy Eggs (60-70 needed to activate all 4 eggs per cycle, up to 100 if unlucky); normal monsters otherwise don't drop hammers, making them hard to farm at scale. Ranked sources: hunting bosses with a Double-Drop general (e.g. Bivals + King's Ring + Hurricane Bird, ~55% double-drop) gives roughly a 50% hammer drop rate regardless of boss level - a survey found 44 drops from 85 kills (51%) across Lv1-8 bosses (Zombie through Peryton, 2-7 hammers per 10 kills each) - so it's faster to farm low-level bosses at the map edge than to chase high-level ones; Vikings drop hammers less often (~28%, 14 hammers from 50 Easy Vikings). Gathering resource tiles Lv6+ also yields hammers, roughly proportional to tile level - a Lv6 tile gives about a 70% chance per 1-2 hour gather (higher-level tiles have better odds but take 5-12 hours and are harder to find repeatedly). Raise gather speed via a high-Political-stat general, a gathering-speed skill general (e.g. Lucy), Monarch Gear crystals equipped before arrival, and the 'increase all resource gathering speed' city buff item.

title: How to get Medals efficiently
summary: Medals ranked: Traded Goods event (500), Viking event (~182-300), and hunting low-level monsters/bosses (always drop, but Ifrit+ and Lava Turtle/Witch/Bayard do not).
content: Ranked medal sources: Traded Goods event gives 500 medals for 200 replacement items, earned via daily quotas (resource production, Alliance Donation, stamina consumption, troop recovery, alliance rally participation, and healing wounded troops) - easy to hit playing daily; Viking event gives 182 medals for defeating 50 Easy Vikings (3 medals/body Lv1-19, 4/body Lv20-49, 5/body Lv50), up to ~300 with a high double-drop general; hunting low-level normal monsters/bosses always drops medals regardless of level - Lv1 Zombie through Lv10 Griffin give 2-5 medals each, Junior/Senior/Excellent Garuda give 5/8/20, Royal Thief gives 4. High-level bosses (Ifrit and above) do NOT drop medals, nor do Lava Turtle, Witch, or Bayard. Non-free: Black Market sells Medal x100 for 7,000 gems and x200 for 12,000 gems.

title: How to raise Monarch Level quickly (How to get Monarch EXP)
summary: Monarch EXP caps General level; best source is Shrine Offering with free Tributes (up to 10M/week), plus Hydra kills and resource tiles Lv13+.
content: Monarch EXP raises Monarch level, which caps how far Generals can be leveled. Best F2P method: Shrine Offering - up to 10,000,000 EXP/week possible at Shrine Lv35 with 6+ gem-paid offers/day (cost escalates 25/50/100/100/200/200 gems, resetting daily) plus free 'Tribute' offerings and Monarch Talent 'Offering' for +20% (max at monarch Lv5). Tributes come mainly from Relics (best - collect 25 fragments, break the tile with a hammer for a chance at 10), then Alliance Shop (10,000 points each), then Black Market (300 gems/5 or 500 gems/10), also Wheel of Fortune/Lv2 Candy - roughly 100-180 tributes/week obtainable this way. Other ranked sources: Crazy Eggs event 230,000+ EXP/day (~12-day event); killing Hydra always gives EXP - chest gives 5,000-17,000 (Lv1-5) plus a flat 3,500 EXP per kill regardless of chest; normal monsters Lv31+ give 2,600 EXP each (Lv30 and below not worth farming); Resource Tiles Lv13-15 give 10,000 EXP each, Lv16 gives 20,000; Dwarf's Lucky Apple gives 22,500 EXP if lucky. Battlefield Shop/Black Market EXP purchases are explicitly not recommended (bad value).

title: How to get Skill Book
summary: Common skillbooks best farmed via Relics, Gather Troops event chests, and Viking Challenge; Debuff via Bacchus Tavern gacha; Duty mostly gem-shop/paid only.
content: Common skill books (Military/Development/Subordinate City) ranked F2P sources: Relics - collect 25 fragments for a chance at a Lv4/Lv3 skillbook or a Skill Book Page (best way to get a Lv4 book without paying); Gather Troops event - completing quotas gives a Luxury Treasure Chest per tier, each always containing 1 Skill Book Page (10 pages = 1 Lv4 book), daily players get 10+ chests; Viking Challenge event - Easy 50 Vikings gives Lv3 x1, Normal 50 gives Lv3 x2, Hard 50 gives Lv4 x1 (needs ~100,000 tier-10 cavalry); disassembling unwanted skillbooks gives 1/2/3/4 pages for Lv1/2/3/4 respectively (10 pages = 1 Lv4 book); Wheel of Fortune in Tavern can randomly give a Lv1 book or Skill Book Chest; Academy Skill Books Shop sells only low-level books for gems (Lv1 3,000g, Lv2 5,000g, Lv3 8,000g). Debuff skill books mainly come from the Bacchus Tavern gacha event (collect Bacchus Gold Coins, redeem a Debuff Skill Book Chest for a random Lv1-4) or a $99.99 paid package. Duty skill books: Academy shop sells Lv1 only, limited to a specific building list, for 3,000 gems, or $99.99 paid packages for Lv1-4.

title: How to get Adv City Teleporter a lot
summary: Adv Teleporters (2,000 gems in store) best farmed via Gather Troops event's Destructive Attack (up to 32) and the Crazy Egg event (4+/day).
content: Adv City Teleporter moves a city to any chosen coordinate (the regular City Teleporter is random-destination only); store price is 2,000 gems each. Ranked F2P sources: Gather Troops event Day 5 'Destructive Attack' gives up to 32 total for cumulatively killing enemy troops (5,000 kills=1pc up to 500,000 kills=10pc; NPC/BoG/BoC kills don't count) - a common alliance trick is reinforcing an alliance city then having an outside sub-account attack it weakly so every reinforcer gets credited; Crazy Egg event gives 4+/day (smashing the 3-hammer egg gives 4pcs, activating the 8-hammer egg gives 2pcs); Dwarf's Lucky Apple gives 10pcs per lucky 5-coin 'Blessing' pull; Daily login + 7 weekly Shrine offerings (unlocks Advanced Daily Rewards) gives 1/week; Trade Products event gives 2pcs (40 exchange items each); Monarch Competition Stage 5 'Kill Enemies' gives 2pcs; Viking Challenge event gives a low-probability chance via the post-trial Viking War Chest; Wheel of Fortune gives a random chance. Note: the Gather Troops event has reportedly become less frequent/reliable over time.

title: How to Get Runestone
summary: Runestones raise General Specialty; best F2P sources are Dwarf's Lucky Apple, Battlefield personal-score rewards, Daily Activity, Champion loyalty, and selling generals.
content: Runestones raise a General's Specialty level. Ranked F2P sources: Dwarf's Lucky Apple - giving a pie to the world-map Witch yields a 'Witch's Gift' with a chance at 100-runestone chests (one player got 300 from 6 gifts); Battlefield personal-score rewards at BoC/BoG/Chalons give 40-350 chests depending on score and win/loss; defeating an enemy player in Battlefield/Server War where both sides lose 30M+ troop power drops runestones; Daily Activity (145 score) gives 5 chests; Champion loyalty level gives escalating chests from Lv1 (3) up to Lv20 (3,000); selling (normal 'Dismiss', not 'Perfectly Dismiss') a trained general refunds runestones - scales steeply with rarity/stars/cultivation, e.g. a Lv38 5-star Gold Historic general with cultivate 500 and Purple x3 Specialty returns 746 chests. Non-free: Auction (40,000 gems minimum bid for 500), Black Market (2,500 gems for 10), paid Advanced Journey event ($4.99 for 1,000) and Event Packages. Chest drop rates by specialty: Leadership 33.85%, Military 30.77%, Glory 24.62%, Immortal 10.77%.

title: How to get VIP Time
summary: VIP level needs active VIP Time for buffs; cheapest recurring source is watching the Black Market for 'VIP 30 Days' at 3,000 gems.
content: VIP level must have active VIP Time to grant its buffs (leveling up only grants 24h automatically). Recommended F2P-friendly method: watch the Black Market for 'VIP 30 Days' (3,000 gems) - it appears at random but reliably shows up at least once before the prior 30-day window expires, and 3,000 gems is achievable within 30 days from other sources. Black Market prices: VIP 30 Minutes = 3,000 resources, VIP 60 Minutes = 6,000 resources, VIP 1 Day = 190 gems, VIP 7 Days = 990 gems, VIP 30 Days = 3,000 gems. Store prices are worse: VIP 1 Day = 250 gems, VIP 7 Days = 1,500 gems, VIP 30 Days = 4,000 gems. Wheel of Fortune can randomly award 'VIP 60 Minutes' (not shown on the visible reward list but confirmed to hit). Purchase Packages sometimes bundle VIP Time cheaply, e.g. a $0.99 200-Gem Speed Up Package includes VIP 1 Day, and a $4.99 Super Value Activity Package includes VIP 1 Day.

title: How to raise Rank quickly (how to get Prestige efficiently)
summary: Prestige comes mostly from Shrine offerings (~100K/week) and boss hunting (~150K/week, bigger weight); Lv4+ normal bosses give the most per kill.
content: Prestige determines Rank; raising Rank also requires a high position in the server's Rank Ranking. The guide author earns ~250,000 prestige/week: ~100,000 from Shrine offerings, ~150,000 from boss hunting (boss hunting carries the bigger weight). Offering method: 6+ gem-paid offers/day (25/50/100/100/200/200 gems, resets daily) plus Monarch Talent 'Offering' for +20% (max monarch Lv5), plus saving 20+ free Tributes and spending them all at once weekly (ideally just before the Sunday Rank Ranking reset) - prestige per offer maxes at the 16th offer (+500 each, 1,500 gems). Tributes come from Relics (best/free, 25-fragment tile breaks), Alliance Shop (10,000 points each), Black Market (300 gems/5, 500 gems/10). Boss hunting: kill Lv4+ 'Normal' bosses for 120-500 prestige each (Lv15 Phoenix=500 max); most Event bosses give only 50 (same as a Lv1 Zombie); normal roaming monsters cap at 65 (Lv40 Scythian Knight); Viking event nets 2,260 (Easy)/2,996 (Normal)/3,730 (Hard) prestige for 50 kills, comparable to killing 10 Lv7-10 bosses.

title: Material Chest & Bag Contents
summary: Lists the 12 crafting-material types and which tiered chests/bags (White/Green/Blue/Purple, Small/Intermediate/Large, Silk Road bag) drop which levels.
content: Material chests/bags drop crafting materials used for equipment forging: 12 types exist per level - Purple Crystal, Blue Stone, Red Agate, Silver Pearl, Meteorolite, Iron, Bronze, Wood, Animal Bone, Leather, Feather, Dragon Scale. Small Material Chest gives 1-2 items from Lv1-2 materials; Intermediate Material Chest gives 1-2 items from Lv2-3; Large Material Chest gives 1-2 items from Lv3-4. Single-tier chests give exactly 1 item each: White Material Chest (Lv1 only), Green Material Chest (Lv2 only), Blue Material Chest (Lv3 only), Purple Material Chest (Lv4 only). Material Bag (Silk Road) gives 1 item from the full Lv5 set (all 12 types). Two restricted Lv5 bags exist: Persia King's Treasure gives 1 item from only Wood/Animal Bone/Leather/Feather/Dragon Scale, and Pharaoh's Treasure gives 1 item from only Meteorolite/Iron/Bronze/Dragon Scale. Main farming source per the wiki: hunting bosses (especially Vikings) drops these chests.

title: Alliance Shop Item List
summary: Alliance-point shop: Tribute costs a fixed 10,000 points (best recurring buy); Monthly-Card section (paid) adds Blood of Ares, Dragon Coins, Teleporters, etc.
content: Alliance Shop is bought with alliance points earned by killing monsters and similar alliance activity. Monthly Card section (requires $19.99/month Alliance Monthly Card) has Regular Exhibits always available - Tribute x5 for 20,000 points, VIP 100 Points x5 for 75,000 points - plus 4 Random Exhibits rotating daily from a pool including Blood of Ares x10 (1,500,000 points), Excellent Dragon Coins x5 (8,800,000), Runestone Chest x100 (600,000), City Teleporter x15 (225,000), and Historic General Fragment x6 (800,000, one of Alfred the Great/Hannibal/Carus/Edward the Black Prince/Kusunoki Masashige/Barbarossa/etc). Normal Items require no payment: Daily Sale (resets daily) includes 10/50/100 Stamina at 30,000/50,000/100,000 points, VIP 100 Points at 15,000, Avatar Change at 10,000, and Tribute at a fixed 10,000 points (recommended buy - price never changes); Weekly Sale (resets weekly) includes 8-Hour/24-Hour/3-Day Truce Agreements at 30,000/50,000/100,000 points. Limited-time discounts (24h validity) appear randomly as Tavern activity progresses - buy discounted items when they appear.

title: Black Market Item List
summary: Rotating gem shop, level-gated; best buys are 24h Speedups and Tribute (resource-cost), worst are Stamina and Monarch/General EXP.
content: Black Market stock rotates and refreshes; some items are gated behind market building level. Key F2P-relevant buys (gem-cost unless noted): 24-Hour Resource Production Speedup x1/x2/x5 cost only resources (recommended always-buy), x20 costs 3,800 gems; Tribute x1=30,000 resources, x5=300 gems, x10=500 gems (always recommended - best monarch-EXP value in the game); RSS packages: gold-buyable up to 5M for 1,763,000 gold, or gem-buyable at best rate 360 gems/M (500K or 500M tiers) - market level 30+ reportedly removes the gold-purchase option for RSS per user reports. VIP Time: 30 Days for 3,000 gems is the best VIP value overall. Not recommended: Stamina (50 for 300 gems - the store's 200 gems/unit up to 10 is cheaper; Shopping Spree event beats both at 800 gems/5), and Monarch/General EXP purchases (worse value than farming). Other notable stock: Medal x100/x200 (7,000/12,000 gems), Refining Stone x10/x50 (1,500/7,500 gems), Runestone Chest x10 (2,500 gems), Dragon Crystal x10 (3,500 gems), Scroll chests (6,075-54,000 gems), Gear Lv3/Lv4 (cheaper than store except Lv3 Intellect/Strength Crown), and gold-cost Equipment tiers Gold1/Gold2/Orange/Purple (120,000/28,200/10,400/3,700 gems) - dragon equipment never appears here.

title: How to increase March Size
summary: Levers that raise march size: Rally Spot level, research, VIP, rank, monarch gear, generals, equipment, items.
content: March size = Rally Spot base value (Lv1 800 -> Lv45 860,000) plus % buffs from other sources, all applied to that base; Free/no-spend levers: Academy research Coordination/Adv Coordination/Super/Supreme Coordination (up to +100,000 and +100%), Military Academy Siege/Melee/Charge Formation research; Rank via prestige (Knight +5,000 up to Regent +100,000); VIP4+ gives +4,000 (VIP4) scaling to +160,000 (VIP25), but requires VIP level 4+ AND an active VIP time item; General march-size skillbook Lv1-4 (+3% to +12%); generals with innate march size skill (King Arthur +15%, Genghis Khan +15%, Barbarossa +10% (Lv4 general skill only), Li Shimin +10%, others); +10-14% more from raising a general's star level to 8, +6-16% from specialty; Duty Officer assigned to Rally Spot (requires Rally Spot Lv35) up to +30%/+70,000, or Toyotomi Hideyoshi/Zhang Liang up to +21-22%; Civilization Treasure 'Imperial Seal' unlock/upgrade (Lv1 +3% up to Lv15 +74.5%); Ideal Land ornaments (+250 to +5,000 each, don't need to be placed); Citizen assigned to Rally Spot, star-leveled (Gold/Purple only), up to +48,000; Guerrilla Tactics grade breakthrough at Rally Spot (+30,000 to +160,000, ground marches only); Paid/time-limited: March Size Increase items (Junior +50%, Medium +100%, Senior +200%, all 1hr; Battlefield Super +300% for 5 min); general equipment (rings/bracers, solo +11-25%, set bonuses +10-30%); Spiritual Beast (Behemoth King etc, up to +26% at max level/quality) and Dragon (Celtic Demon +10-20%, Amaterasu Dragon Blade +15% at Lv40, Nidhogg +20% at Lv40).

title: Maximum March Size (by keep level)
summary: Reference march-size totals for keep levels 26-40, broken down by rally spot, research, VIP, rank, gear, item use.
content: Approximate 'typical' max march size by keep level, assuming a normal (not maxed) progression of Rally Spot level, research, VIP, rank, and monarch gear for that keep level, plus optional March Size Increase item use (0/50/100/200%); NOT a true theoretical maximum since VIP/rank/monarch gear can exceed keep-level norms. Examples (no item used / with 200% item): k26 298,200 / 518,200; k28 492,200 / 782,200; k30 772,400 / 1,172,400; k32 971,400 / 1,471,400; k34 1,313,400 / 1,943,400; k36 1,769,500 / 2,539,500; k38 2,326,550 / 3,246,550; k40 3,427,500 / 4,527,500. At higher keep levels the breakdown increasingly includes General Equipment (Fearless Dragon/Ares Ring, Plantagenet Ring), March Size skillbook Lv4 (+12%), Dragon Celtic Demon (Lv6-10), Rally Spot Duty Officer (Special Officer +25%/+50,000, Toyotomi Hideyoshi +5%), Civilization Treasure Imperial Seal, and Ideal Land ornaments. Full derivation and all lever details are in 'How to increase March Size'.

title: How to increase March Speed
summary: 3 march-speed types (basic, to-monster, to-resource) and the free/paid levers that raise each.
content: 3 march speed types: (A) Basic march speed applies everywhere; (B) March Speed to Monsters (also speeds the return trip, even as rally owner/participant); (C) March Speed to Resource Tile. Items: 'March Speed Increase' +20% for 1hr (1,250 Gems, buyable from Store); paid Adv (+40%)/Super (+40-60%) versions via IAP; single-march speed items (+30-50%, stackable) let you strike distant enemies almost instantly. (A) Basic levers: General Leadership (level/enhance/cultivate/dragon), skillbook Lv1-4 (+10-25%), specific generals (Seleucus I +35%, Huo Qubing -20% march time), Covenant (up to +155% applying to ALL generals if multiple covenant generals are maxed), Research (Adv Compass up to +70%, Raid/Adv Raid up to +100% combined), Monarch Gear Courage Horn (Lv6 +33%), Monarch Talent Lv2 pick, Spiritual Beast Thunder Scorpion (up to +30%), 4-beast March Speed Seals (up to +120% combined at Lv20), Dragon (Fasolt +18.8% at Lv40 for 8hrs, Fafnir up to +40% at Lv20 for that general only), Rank (Knight +5% up to Regent +70%), VIP13-25 (+10% to +50%), Art Hall Discobolus (+10% to +60%), Alliance Science donation buff, Victory Column Lv3-5 (requires Keep 35; -10/-20/-30% march time), Coronation Fountain Zeus's Might (up to ~+13%), Champion Josephine/Nefertiti intimacy (up to +60% each, +120% both maxed). (B) March Speed to Monsters levers (separate, larger buffs): skillbook Lv1-4 (+15-45%), Research Tracing/Master Tracker (up to +150% combined), Monarch Gear Justice Horn (Lv6 +66%), 7-beast Seals (up to +296% combined at Lv20), VIP7-25 (+30% to +150%), Art Hall Discobolus (+15% to +120%), Civilization Treasure Tutankhamun's Dagger (Lv1 +10% up to Lv15 +350%), paid subscriptions (Alliance Monthly Card +40%/$19.99/30d, Super Value Privilege Card +15%/$49.99/365d), event-only Blessing of Flame/Pan/Wind (+50%/$4.99/1day), Coronation Fountain Poseidon's Command (up to +30%). (C) To Resource Tile: Research Gathering March Speed (up to +10%) and Adv Gathering March Speed.

title: How to increase the General's Power (and the Danger)
summary: 5 levers that raise a general's power score, plus the key warning that power != actual strength.
content: General power is driven by: (1) Stats (leadership/attack/defense/politics) via leveling up, enhance (star level), Cultivate (up to ~+500 total; gold covers up to ~+300, gems needed beyond that; roughly +1 net stat per 10 Cultivate attempts on average, so ~3,000 Cultivates needed to reach +300), and assigning a dragon (+~6 to +100 depending on dragon level); (2) Specialty, unlocked at general level 25, with 5 quality tiers (Green->Blue->Purple->Orange->Gold) that each add more power; (3) Star Level: below star 5 power rises via stat gains; from star 6+ each red star itself adds a large power jump (e.g. Red Star 1 ~150,000 power, Red Star 2 ~180,000, Red Star 3 ~200,000) independent of stats; (4) Generals' Equipment: pricier gear + star-upgrading (up to 5 stars, 3-10x power) + refining adds the most power (first refine ~1.5x; top-tier Achaemenidae gear can reach +100% buff total per piece via refine); (5) Spiritual Beast and Dragon assignment also raise a general's power (dragon refining adds +60-100% depending on dragon type; power shown on the dragon's own screen does not reflect the boost given to the general). THE DANGER: power is only a rough guide, NOT equal to real strength; skillbook buffs, research buffs, and monarch gear buffs do NOT count toward the power number at all, so a general fully buffed via research/skillbooks can be far stronger than their power score suggests, and conversely a general decked in expensive but wrong-type (unrefined) equipment can have high power yet be weaker than a cheaply-equipped general with correctly-refined, type-matched gear.

title: How to increase the Max Number of Generals you can hold
summary: General storage cap rises with Keep level and with VIP level (using VIP time); limits have been raised repeatedly.
content: The maximum number of generals you can hold (shown atop the Generals screen) is increased by two free levers: (1) Keep level — cap rises as you upgrade the Keep, e.g. Keep 28 -> 50 generals, Keep 29 -> 52, Keep 33 -> 67, Keep 39 -> 104; (2) VIP level plus an active VIP time item — each VIP level adds generals to the cap (VIP1 +0, VIP2 +1, VIP3 +2, ... up to VIP23 +70, VIP24 +75, VIP25 +81); if VIP time lapses, the effective cap drops and you may show as over-capacity (e.g. '75/67') without losing any generals — they remain, you just can't add more until you free slots or VIP time is reactivated. These caps have been raised multiple times via game updates (June 2021, November 2021, April 2024), each time retroactively increasing the limit for existing players.

title: How to get more March Slot
summary: March slots start at 1, max out at 6: +4 from free Academy research, +1 from VIP5+ with VIP time active.
content: March slots (simultaneous marches) start at 1 and cap at 6 total. Free path via Academy research (+4 max, all one-time, no ongoing cost once researched): Military > Formation (requires Academy Lv6) +1; Military > Adv Dispatch (requires Academy Lv13) +1; Military > Super Adv Dispatch (requires Academy Lv25) +1; Military Advance > Legion Expansion (requires Academy Lv35) +1. VIP path: reaching VIP level 5+ AND spending/activating a VIP time item grants +1 march slot while VIP time is active; this bonus does not increase further at any higher VIP level (even VIP20+), it is capped at a flat +1. So the practical maximum is 1 (base) + 4 (research) + 1 (VIP) = 6 march slots.

title: VIP Guide
summary: Overview: VIP level unlocks benefits, earned via VIP Points from in-game activity; benefits require VIP Time to be active.
content: Every player has a VIP Level that rises by earning VIP Points through various in-game activities (see 'How to increase VIP Level & earn VIP Points'). Higher VIP Level unlocks a wide variety of benefits/perks (detailed per-level in 'List of VIP Buffs & Benefits by VIP Level'). Critically, VIP benefits are NOT automatically passive: to actually receive the VIP buffs at your level, your 'VIP Time' must be enabled/active (consumed via VIP time items — see 'How to get VIP Time'); reaching a VIP level alone does not grant its buffs if VIP Time has expired.

title: List of VIP Buffs & Benefits by VIP Level
summary: Full VIP1-28 buff table: VIP5 march slot, VIP8 construction+, VIP10 research+, VIP17 free builder, VIP14+ monster double-drop; buffs need active VIP Time.
content: Full per-level VIP buff table (VIP1-28); VIP buffs apply only while a VIP Time item is active. Economy-relevant thresholds (cumulative, gem-free once VIP level reached): All Resources Production VIP2 +1% -> VIP10 +10% -> VIP15 +20% -> VIP20 +35% -> VIP28 +70%; Free Construction Speed-Up grant scales VIP1 +1min -> VIP7 +10min -> VIP15 +36min -> VIP20 +1:20 -> VIP28 +4:30 (a free periodic speedup source); Construction Speed VIP8 +10% -> VIP15 +18% -> VIP20 +30% -> VIP28 +54%; Crafting Speed from VIP9 +10%; Research Speed VIP10 +10% -> VIP15 +20% -> VIP20 +30% -> VIP28 +46%; Healing Speed from VIP11 +20%; Training Speed VIP12 +10% -> VIP18 +25% -> VIP20 +35% -> VIP24 +45%; March Speed from VIP13 +10%. FREE BUILDER +1 from VIP17 onward (an extra construction queue as long as VIP17+ is active). March Slot +1 at VIP5; March Size Capacity VIP4 +4,000 -> VIP15 +20,000 -> VIP20 +50,000 -> VIP28 +210,000; March Preset Slots 3/4/5/6/7/8 at VIP5/10/13/15/18/20. General Capacity VIP2 +1 -> VIP10 +17 -> VIP15 +34 -> VIP20 +55 -> VIP28 +104. Free daily levies/taxes scale: Gold Levy Times +5(VIP1)->+140(VIP28), Free Tax Times +3(VIP3)->+13(VIP28). Stamina Recovery VIP6 +25% -> VIP15 +50% -> VIP20 +100%; March Speed to Monsters VIP7 +30% -> VIP20 +100% -> VIP25 +150%; Double Items Drop from Monsters VIP14 +10% -> VIP20 +40% -> VIP25 +50%. Subordinate City capacity +1/+2/+3 at VIP15/18/26; VIP13 unlocks Black Market Repurchase (+5/day rebuy); VIP15+ adds combat Atk/Def/HP buffs; VIP27/28 Rally Capacity +6%/+12%. VIP Points required are cumulative and steep: VIP10=77,200, VIP15=1,157,500, VIP20=23,148,200, VIP25=77,160,500, VIP28=240,000,000.

title: How to buy packs at least 10% cheaper
summary: Mostly-defunct method: pay via Amazon Coins for up to 10% off; Amazon Coins and the Aptoide workaround are largely discontinued now.
content: NOTE: this discount method is largely no longer available — Amazon Coins have been discontinued and the Aptoide (AppCoins) workaround is basically unusable now (only some old installs still work). When it worked: buying Amazon Coins in bulk gave a discount (e.g., as of 4/2023, 50,000 coins cost $450 instead of $500 = 10% off; smaller amounts gave 3-8% off), then those coins paid for in-game purchases inside the Amazon Appstore version of Evony (not the regular iOS/Android app). Setup required installing 'Amazon Appstore' (Android only, or via an Android emulator like BlueStacks on PC/Mac for iOS players), installing Evony from it, and switching to your existing account via a previously-linked Facebook or Google account. Remaining live tip: for players billed in non-USD currency, per-platform (iOS vs Android) pricing still differs and which is cheaper varies seasonally, so compare both before buying.

title: Paid packages: the best value for which ones to buy and when?
summary: For <$100/month spenders: buy one $4.99 Event Package/day, prioritize Limited Time Promotion + weekend bonus tiers.
content: For budgets under ~$100/month, best value rule of thumb: buy only when a wanted bonus appears in 'Limited Time Promotion'; buy only 'Event Package' or 'Super Value Activity Package' (skip Membership/Bounty Cave, poor value); buy only during the 3-day weekend (extra Weekend Purchase Package bonus); cap spend at one $4.99 pack/day. That's roughly $20/month buying weekly, ~$60/month buying 3x/week. Limited Time Promotion: 4 package types (Speed Up, Resources, Materials & Skills, Research) rotate hourly, in 3 purchase tiers (Tier1 ~1,000 gems/$5, Tier2 ~24,000 gems/$100, Tier3 ~100,000 gems/$400), max 1 of each tier/day; 'Speed Up' is most useful until mid-game, 'Materials and Skills' has the best rate of return (best late-game), 'Resources' is NOT recommended (poor redemption rate). Event Package ($4.99 base: 26,000 Gems, 3M Gold, 23.5M Resources x4 types, ~4 days speedups, resurrection/research/enhancement items) escalates through 5 grades same-day ($4.99->$9.99->$19.99->$49.99->$99.99, each requires buying the prior grade first that day; max 1 of grades 1-4/day, up to 12 of grade 5/day); over time these packages help stockpile Source of Life, Runestone, and Research Stone. Super Value Activity Package (unlocked at 120 daily activity score, 1/day) gives ~15 days speedups, 26,000 Gems, 2.5M Gold, 7.5M Resources x4, but no Source of Life/Runestone — use it when you need speedups urgently. Weekend Purchase Package: bonus tier based on total gems bought that weekend (500 gems->Level1 up to 70,000 gems->Level10); one $4.99 pack reaches Level 2.

title: TIPS for Intentionally Creating Wounded Troops (make as many as you need)
summary: Monster battles cap wounds at 10% per fight; attack with 10x the wounded troops you want and lose on purpose to hit event goals exactly.
content: Core mechanic: in Evony monster/boss battles, a single battle will never wound more than 10% of the troops sent (verified). Exploit: send 10x the number of troops you want wounded and deliberately lose — e.g., to get exactly 200 wounded troops, attack a monster with 2,000 troops and lose on purpose. Use non-ground troop types for a reliable 10% cap (ground-only troops reportedly can wound at less than 10%). If the Monarch Talent 'Mortality' (Lv6) is selected, the wound cap drops to 5%, so you then need 20x the troops instead of 10x. Primary use case: events that reward you for healing a set number of troops — e.g. trade-goods events (Eagle of Freedom, Strong Drink, Shining Treasure, Silk Road, Fragrance, Tea Culture) where healing 30,000 troops yields 300 exchange items, and 200 of those exchange for 500 medals — plus the daily 'heal troops' activity score, and pre-staging wounded troops before events like the Monarch Competition 'Power Increase' stage.

title: What is "Adv Dispatch" ?
summary: Adv Dispatch is an Academy research item (Military category) that grants +1 march slot per level and gates the Keep 14 upgrade.
content: 'Adv Dispatch' is a Military-category research item at the Academy, NOT an auto-gather/auto-dispatch feature — per this source, leveling it up grants +1 march slot per level. It gates progress: you cannot upgrade the Keep to level 14 until 'Adv Dispatch Level 1' is researched (shown in red on the Keep upgrade screen if missing). Requirements to start researching Adv Dispatch: Academy Level 13, 900,000 Gold, and these prerequisite research levels already completed: Conscription Lv2, Swordplay Lv2, Infantry Armor Lv2, Feathered Arrow Lv2, Archer Armor Lv2, Formation Lv1, Horseback Riding Lv3, Cavalry Armor Lv3, Battering Ram Lv3, Siege Machine Armor Lv3, Tracing Lv3, Ground/Mounted/Ranged/Siege Troop HP Lv4 each, and Coordination Lv4. (Note: total march slots — including Adv Dispatch's contribution — are also capped/interact with VIP level; see 'How to get more March Slot' for the full +4 research / +1 VIP breakdown.)

title: Auction House – Product lists, Winning bid prices, etc
summary: 3 auction tiers (Mysterious/Rare/Common) traded for Gems; anti-snipe 5-min extension; gems refunded but must be manually claimed.
content: The Auction House sells 3 tiers of goods, all bid/bought with Gems: Mysterious Goods (all servers compete against each other; bid price freely set above the 'Lowest Increment'; no Buy-It-Now; examples: Saint Michael Castle ~20.4-22M, Famous City Key (Germany) ~35.1-45M, various Scroll Fragments 4.2M-16.9M); Rare Goods (only a few servers compete; freely-set bid price, no Buy-It-Now; examples: Epic Historic General (Premium) ~150-200K, 5K VIP Points x5 ~50-80K typical sale, Source of Life x100 ~55K); Common Goods (only a few servers compete, bid price is auto-set by the system, DOES have a Buy-It-Now price; examples: 5M Resource Box x4 ~9K, 100 Stamina x10 ~16.5K). Mechanics: Gems are charged at the moment you place a bid; if outbid, gems are refunded automatically but must be manually collected via the 'Claim' button (bottom right); any bid placed within the last 5 minutes resets the countdown to 5 minutes (anti-sniping). Strategy value: a source for VIP Points, Source of Life, premium/historic generals, and bulk resources not reliably available elsewhere, but gems are committed upfront and item appearance timing (e.g., premium generals) appears random/unscheduled.

title: Evony: The King's Return GUIDE WIKI
summary: Roster of Evony's recurring limited-time events (category index page).
content: Category listing of recurring Evony events (19 posts), no single timing table given here (see each event's own page for cadence): Bacchus Tavern; Gladiator's Trial quiz; Revelation of Horus; Triumphal Celebration; Hecate's Moon; Battlefield/Clash of Civilizations (CoC); Clash of Civilizations Assault (Shadow of Dawn); Lucky Composing; Treasure Hunt (Pyramid, ~10 days); Revelation of Maya; Alliance Competition (~10 days); Crazy Eggs; Dwarf's Lucky Apple; Arctic Barbarians Invasion; Cleopatra's Treasure; Consuming Return; World Boss; Dawn of Civilization/Monarch Sciences (~10 days, ~2x/month); King's Party/Royal Party (~10 days, ~2x/month); Kill Event (KE). Most limited-time events run about 10 days and several recur roughly twice a month; a bot should track each event's own start/end rather than assume a fixed shared schedule.

title: King's Party / Royal Party
summary: Gem-spending 'cake' event; no free/action-based scoring, purely Basic Gem purchases.
content: Cadence: held periodically, about twice a month, duration usually ~10 days (sometimes shorter). Mechanic: purchasing Basic Gems (not bonus gems bundled in packages) raises a 'cake' level; only base gem spend counts, e.g. a $4.99 package counts only its 1,000 base gems, not the 20,000 bonus gems. Cumulative Basic Gems needed: Lv2 5,000 (~$20) up to Lv10 500,000 (~$2,000). Rewards: Lv4 Civilization Equipment Scroll, Lv8 Duty Officer Equipment Scroll, Lv10 Civilization Equipment Scroll + castle decoration + epic general. Spending 200,000 gems unlocks Royal Party (next tier, up to Lv7 ~1,250,000 gems). This event has no free-to-play scoring path and does not reward build/gather/kill/speedup actions directly -- it is pure pay-to-progress, relevant to a bot only as a spending sink to skip, not a target to farm.

title: Treasure Hunt Event (Pyramid)
summary: ~10-day event: explore map pyramids (20/day) for score; free players can hit 500M+ for 189K gems.
content: Event period ~10 days. Personal score target 500M nets 189,000 gems total (chest tiers from 5M to 500M score); unpaid players commonly exceed 2,000M. Scoring action: send troops to explore pyramids appearing on the map (Lv1-5, found ~3x/hour plus extra); costs 20 stamina and a daily cap of 20 entries; exploration time fixed by level (5-25 min) regardless of troops/general used. Score and resources/items returned are random but average by level: Lv3 ~1.5M, Lv4 ~10M, Lv5 ~20M. Send a general-less force of ~100,000 T1 troops (carry capacity ~1.3M) -- more/higher-level troops do not raise score. Dive alone (not with alliance-mates) to maximize personal score, since rewards split among all who enter one pyramid. Raising Alliance 'Mysterious Relic Exploration' research increases items/resources brought back, directly raising score. Top-10 monarch rewards: castle decorations, 50K-300K gems, alliance honor, dragon source fragments.

title: Wisdom Dome
summary: Late-game building (Keep 28+) unlocking Goddess Attendant troop-type buff books at set levels.
content: Not a timed event but a permanent building requiring Keep level 28 to start. Milestones unlock equippable 'Goddess Attendant' buff books: Lv2 Charge (Mounted), Lv5 Precision (Ranged), Lv9 Formation (Ground), Lv12 Siegecraft (Siege), Lv15 Missile (Ranged & Siege). Upgrade costs scale from ~2.05M/2.7M/2.7M/2.7M (food/lumber/stone/ore) at Lv1 up to tens of billions of each resource by Lv23-25, with construction speed/power also rising sharply (power 158,000 at Lv1 to 55,182,000 at Lv25); total Goddess Attendant boost reaches +20% at Lv25 (requires Keep 50). For a bot, this is a long-horizon base_dev target gated by keep level and resource stockpiling rather than an event to farm, relevant mainly for planning when to queue construction given the required Keep tier.

title: Server Gifts on Wall – Item List
summary: Free hourly + random server-wall items; no action needed beyond claiming, so a bot should always collect.
content: Two free item sources on the city wall, no action/points required beyond claiming: (1) a fixed hourly item at each server-time hour (0:00-23:00), rotating speedups such as 3 Hour Speedup for Construction, 3 Hour Speedup for Researching, plain 3/8 Hour Speed Up, and periodic VIP minutes (e.g. VIP points boosted at certain hours for higher server-gift levels); (2) untimed random items granted whenever any player on the server buys a package, e.g. Free Tax x10, 30 Minute Speed Up x5, 10K Food/Lumber Box x5, VIP 60 minutes x5. Quantities scale with the server's gift wall level (verified at level 5-12, e.g. higher levels give 3x material chests). A bot should check and claim the wall gift every server hour and whenever notified, since these speedups/resources/VIP time are pure free upside with zero action cost.

title: Dawn of Civilization / Monarch Sciences – How to complete all quests
summary: ~10-day, ~2x/month quest event (40 quests, 3 categories) scored by gathering, killing, building, and speedups.
content: Event period 10 days, held about twice a month. 5,000 total points across 3 categories (Gathering, Alliance, Ambition; 40 quest tiers), rewards every 100 points (50 levels); no per-day theme split stated -- each quest instead has a 10-day target best paced daily. Free-to-play daily paces: gather 6M+/day (60M total), produce 5M+/day (50M total), cultivate generals 23x/day (230 total), refine blue equipment 23x/day, offer in shrine 10x/day (needs pre-saved tribute), rally 16x/day, alliance donate 5x/day, speed up alliance members 10x/day, buy 20 black-market items/day, consume 240 stamina/day (~12 monster kills), train mass T1 troops, heal 80,000 troops (lose fights vs weak/dead cities on purpose), revive 80,000 troop souls (Holy Palace Lv25+ needed), recall 100,000 deserters, increase power 70M, plunder 18M from dead/retired cities. Rewards: march effect (hides general icon while marching), avatar frames, cheap golden historic generals (Aethelflaed, Pyrrhus, Merlin, Marcus Agrippa, Maurice de Saxe, Raimondo, Narses, Mark Antony). Can also buy all 50 levels with gems (10,400/level).

title: Art Hall: Exhibition Hall Reward List
summary: Free reward track: collect matching artwork sets (from chests/drops) across 10 halls for gems, speedups, stamina.
content: Not a timed event -- a permanent Art Hall collection track gated by Art Hall building level (Lv11 to Lv25-30 across the 10 halls). Each hall needs a matching set of 4 artwork items (e.g. Knight's Sword/Leg Armor/Armor/Helmet for Knight Equipment Hall), sourced from Artwork Fragment Chests I-VI (obtained from boss drops, events, chests -- no gems required). Progress thresholds are 20/60/200 items per hall (280 total per hall) unlocking 3 reward tiers each: e.g. Knight Equipment Hall gives 1K-5K Gems + 500K Food Box + 24 Hour Speed Ups; other halls give Research Stone, Stamina, Arrest Warrants, Speedups for Researching/Construction/Crafting, Adv City Teleporters. Because fragments come free from normal boss-killing and chest drops, a bot passively accumulates progress toward these rewards simply by fighting monsters/bosses and opening event chests; no dedicated grinding action is required beyond building Art Hall higher to unlock later halls.

title: Limited Time Promotion – All Package Contents
summary: Paid-only gem package (Lv1-3) for speedups/resources; no free-to-play scoring or earn path.
content: This is a cash-shop 'Limited Time Package' (Lv1-3), not an earnable event -- there is no free/action-based way to progress it. Required Basic Gems: Lv1 1,000, Lv2 24,000, Lv3 100,000. Contents scale with level: Speed Ups from 5/10-minute increments up to a combined ~594 days total across all 3 levels; Food Box 7M/35M/125M (167M total) plus matching Lumber/Stone/Ore boxes (41.5M each total). Completing all 3 levels grants a bonus bundle: 60 Material Bags (Silk Road), 30 Lv4 Skill Book Chests, 100 Lv4 Dragon Scale, 1,000 Refining Stone, 60M Gold, 125M x4 resource boxes, and ~169 days of additional speedups (80x 12hr, 40x 24hr, 10x 3-day). For a bot's purposes this package is purely a spend decision (value-per-dollar), not a task to optimize via gameplay actions.

title: Lucky Composing Event
summary: Composite cards drop from gathering/monster-killing; combine 3 of 5 types into lucky boxes (10 tiers).
content: During this event, composite items (5 card types) drop from resource tiles and from killing monsters -- i.e. a bot's normal gathering and monster-hunting actions passively generate event currency with no extra action needed. Combining any 3 of the 5 card types crafts a 'lucky box'; there are 10 box tiers, higher numbers containing better loot, and box contents vary per event run (not fixed). Strategy to fully use up a card stockpile: repeatedly select the 3 currently-most-numerous card types and combine roughly half the max possible each round, repeating until only a remainder (from non-multiple-of-3 totals) is left; a per-event simulation tool lets players enter card counts and get an optimal combine sequence.
